maintenance.label: Maintenance mode
maintenance.messagePlaceholder: PinTool is temporarily unavailable for upgrades…
maintenance.switchHint: Toggling immediately affects user-facing routes (admin paths bypass).
maintenance.enableDescription: All non-admin requests will receive HTTP 503 until you turn this off.

overview.heading: Overview
overview.nativePostureTitle: How close the platform is to a true private runtime right now.
overview.nativePostureSummary: This dashboard now separates raw admin activity from the actual privacy posture: PER + Umbra deployment coverage, live private controls, and the missing follower-vault execution surfaces still required by the target architecture.
overview.coverageFootnote: Coverage uses the current admin deployment list. Follower-vault runtime objects are not live yet, so this is deployment-level posture only.
overview.missingFollowerVaults: Follower vault inventory, subscription lifecycle, and private copy-trading fan-out are still missing as first-class runtime objects.
overview.missingPerRuntime: PER is not yet the authoritative strategy runtime for creator logic, follower allocations, and private execution traces.
overview.missingUmbraIsolation: Umbra identity isolation is still deployment-centric / shared; target architecture requires per-follower-vault isolation.
overview.missingVisibilityGrants: Selective visibility grants and revocation are not yet fully surfaced as operational admin controls.
overview.rolloutItemVaultsBody: Add global and per-deployment vault inventory with PDA refs, authority refs, and Umbra identity refs.
overview.rolloutItemSubscriptionsBody: Add status, allocation mode, max capital, and visibility preset monitoring for every follower subscription.
overview.rolloutItemCyclesBody: Add accepted / running / failed / recovered monitoring for private fan-out strategy cycles.

users.heading: Users
users.subheading: End-user wallets. Superadmins can ban / unban; bans propagate via the platform guard.
users.banDescription: Type the wallet address to confirm: {wallet}

strategies.heading: Strategies
strategies.subheading: All published, draft and archived strategies (admin scope).
strategies.name: Name
strategies.creator: Creator

deployments.heading: Deployments
deployments.subheading: Operator+: pause / resume / stop. Superadmin: force-close (revokes PER tokens). Privacy drill-downs show what is live now and what follower-vault surfaces are still missing.
deployments.creator: Creator
deployments.confirmDescription: Type the deployment id to confirm: {id}
deployments.detailDescription: Current live deployment metadata plus the target follower-vault rollout that still needs backend support.
deployments.detailGapIntro: This drill-down is still deployment-centric. It does not mean the follower-vault architecture already exists.
deployments.detailGapIdentity: Umbra identity is still platform-scoped today; target is isolated per follower vault.
deployments.detailGapRuntime: PER session data exists, but PER is not yet the full private strategy runtime for copy-trading.
deployments.detailGapVaults: Subscriptions, follower vaults, grants, and private cycles are still planned admin surfaces.
deployments.blueprintSubscriptionNote: Add deployment-scoped subscription inventory with follower status, visibility preset, and allocation configuration references.
deployments.blueprintVaultNote: Add per-deployment follower-vault list with PDA refs, authority refs, Umbra identity refs, and lifecycle status.
deployments.blueprintCycleNote: Add cycle monitoring so operators can see accepted, running, failed, and recovered private fan-out jobs.
deployments.blueprintGrantNote: Add grant inventory and revocation paths for creator, follower-self, and auditor visibility scopes.
deployments.detailNoSubscriptions: No follower subscriptions yet for this deployment.
deployments.detailNoPrivateCycles: No private execution cycles started yet.

privacy.heading: Privacy
privacy.subheading: Current deployment-level control plane plus the target follower-vault native privacy blueprint.
privacy.architectureTitle: Follower vaults stay public only as deterministic control shells.
privacy.architectureSummary: The selected end state keeps PDA vaults as authority anchors, moves sensitive execution into MagicBlock PER, and moves sensitive balances and treasury visibility into Umbra.
privacy.controlShellSummary: Public accounts stay minimal and deterministic so operators can verify lifecycle and control edges without revealing follower internals.
privacy.controlShellBullet1: StrategySubscription PDA and FollowerVault PDA are the public anchors.
privacy.controlShellBullet2: Only lifecycle, authority, and coarse references stay public.
privacy.perRuntimeSummary: PER becomes the private brain for strategy parameters, follower allocation outputs, and private execution traces.
privacy.perRuntimeBullet1: Sensitive strategy logic executes inside the private runtime.
privacy.perRuntimeBullet2: Follower-specific configuration and mirror sizing stay private.
privacy.perRuntimeBullet3: Private execution cycles should fan out follower vault actions.
privacy.umbraTreasurySummary: Umbra becomes the private money layer for encrypted balances, fee accrual, and selective viewing grants.
privacy.umbraTreasuryBullet1: Every follower vault should map to an isolated Umbra identity.
privacy.umbraTreasuryBullet2: Balances and payouts stay encrypted by default.
privacy.umbraTreasuryBullet3: Selective disclosure must be revocable and scoped.
privacy.implementationGapTitle: Why this is not native privacy yet
privacy.implementationGapIntro: These gaps are still structural, even when adapters are online.
privacy.gapSharedIdentity: Umbra still resolves from one platform-level keeper path instead of isolated follower-vault identities.
privacy.gapPerReadPath: PER is still exposed mainly as an auth-gated read surface, not the authoritative strategy runtime.
privacy.gapFollowerRuntime: Follower subscriptions, follower vaults, and private copy-trading cycles are not first-class admin objects yet.
privacy.gapVisibilityGrants: Selective visibility and grant revocation are not surfaced as real admin operations yet.
privacy.tokensRedacted: {n} PER tokens · token values are redacted
privacy.revokeTokenDescription: Type the token prefix to confirm.
privacy.revokeAllDescription: Type the deployment id to confirm: {id}
privacy.targetBlueprintSummary: These modules are the next admin surfaces once follower-vault data, private execution cycles, and visibility grants become real runtime objects.
privacy.targetFollowerVaultsSummary: Track funded, shielded, paused, and exiting follower vaults with PDA refs, authority refs, and private-state refs.
privacy.targetSubscriptionsSummary: Show pending funding, active, paused, and exiting subscriptions without leaking sibling follower internals.
privacy.targetUmbraIdentitiesSummary: Expose whether each follower vault has its own Umbra identity, seed domain, and disclosure policy.
privacy.targetGrantsSummary: Audit bounded disclosure grants, expiry windows, and revocations for creator, follower, and auditor scopes.
privacy.targetCyclesSummary: Monitor accepted, running, failed, and recovered PER cycles without leaking raw strategy outputs.
privacy.backlogFollowerVaults: Global follower-vault list with PDA refs, custody mode, lifecycle status, and isolation indicators.
privacy.backlogSubscriptions: Global subscription read model with follower status, allocation mode, and visibility preset.
privacy.backlogGrants: Visibility grant inventory with scope, grantee, expiry, and revocation status.
privacy.backlogCycles: Private execution cycle monitoring for accepted, running, failed, and recovered strategy cycles.
privacy.backlogDeploymentVaults: Per-deployment follower-vault drill-down used by the deployments page privacy drawer.
privacy.hookFollowerVaults: Frontend list query for follower-vault inventory, filters, and isolation posture badges.
privacy.hookSubscriptions: Frontend read model for active and pending subscriptions plus per-deployment drill-down.
privacy.hookGrants: Frontend visibility-grant table and expiry monitoring surfaces.
privacy.hookCycles: Frontend monitoring hook for PER private-cycle status and failure summaries.
privacy.hookUmbraIdentities: Frontend inventory of current Umbra identity isolation domains and disclosure policies.
privacy.pagePrivacyNote: Keep live adapter health, but add follower-vault, subscription, grants, and private-cycle surfaces as first-class tabs.
privacy.pageDeploymentsNote: Expose deployment-level privacy drill-downs and the target follower-vault rollout checklist.
privacy.pageOverviewNote: Add native-privacy KPIs once follower-vault and private-cycle data exists.
privacy.pageSystemNote: Show PER health, Umbra callback health, and degraded-mode warnings for privacy infrastructure.
privacy.vaultsSubtitle: Per-deployment vault inventory with PDA refs, custody, and lifecycle status. PDA columns starting with 'placeholder-' indicate Phase-1 synthetic identifiers pending the Anchor accounts.
privacy.subscriptionsSubtitle: All follower subscriptions across deployments. Cap is shown in raw token units; allocation mode drives the proportional fan-out at cycle time.
privacy.cyclesSubtitle: Cycle-level orchestration metadata. Receipt payloads are sanitized — operators inspect allocation amounts, never raw signal data.
privacy.identitiesSubtitle: One Umbra signer per follower vault, derived from the keeper master key via HKDF. Only the salt prefix is surfaced; the keeper secret never leaves the keeper service.
privacy.grantsSubtitle: Bounded disclosure ledger. Grants are advisory in this round (DB only); enforcement layers land with the next phase.
privacy.cycleDetailSubheading: Sanitized per-follower receipts for a single cycle.
privacy.cycleDetailNoReceipts: No receipts produced for this cycle.

system.heading: System
system.subheading: Health, adapters, keeper balance and maintenance mode (superadmin only).

toast.allPerTokensRevoked: All PER tokens for deployment revoked
